name: Official civic and public data
accessMode: Public APIs, public maps, public pages
currentReality: Metro records, public data portals, maps, dashboards, agendas, and department pages are the most dependable daily story engine.
whatWeCanDoNow: Scan Metro Planning, Codes, NDOT, MNPD, hubNashville, Council, Parks, Library, and public health sources
Filter by East Nashville zones, ZIPs, council districts, corridors, and watched addresses
Generate confirmed quick hits, explainers, traffic notes, event impacts, and watchdog briefs
whatNeeds: Saved query list for zones and corridors
publishUse: Hard-fact briefs, permit watch, road closures, civic explainers, safety trends, and source checks for rumors.

name: Local web, RSS, and newsletters
accessMode: RSS, search queries, public pages, newsletters
currentReality: Citywide Nashville coverage regularly contains East Nashville items that need local translation, follow-up, and source credit.
whatWeCanDoNow: Run saved searches for East Nashville, Five Points, Gallatin Pike, East Bank, Shelby, Inglewood, Lockeland, Riverside, and McFerrin
Monitor local outlets, food/event publications, neighborhood orgs, and official newsletters
Turn found items into link briefs or follow-up questions instead of rewriting another outlet
publishUse: Link briefs, local angle explainers, source triangulation, and SEO follow-ups.

name: Instagram public account watchlist
accessMode: Public posts, public reels, manual capture, optional approved API/scheduler
currentReality: Public business, venue, restaurant, creator, and civic accounts are useful for openings, closures, menus, events, hiring, and visual leads. Stories are fragile and need quick capture.
whatWeCanDoNow: Build a watchlist of restaurants, bars, coffee shops, venues, pop-ups, parks, neighborhood orgs, council members, and Nashville food/event accounts
Capture public posts with source links and screenshots where needed
Use direct business posts for facts and comments/reposts only as chatter
publishUse: Soft Launch Watch, weekend agenda, restaurant notes, closing/opening leads, and visual story embeds.

name: X public search and official accounts
accessMode: Public search, lists, optional paid API
currentReality: X is useful for breaking links, officials, reporters, transit, traffic, and civic commentary, but random posts are leads, not facts.
whatWeCanDoNow: Create public lists for Metro departments, council members, reporters, venues, food writers, and high-signal Nashville accounts
Run saved keyword searches for East Nashville locations and corridors
Use official/direct posts as sources and everything else as a pointer
publishUse: Breaking pointers, official quote trails, link discovery, and source triangulation.

name: East Nashville private Facebook group
status: No clean API; supervised capture only
accessMode: Kenny-selected post capture, screenshots, links, and source notes
automationReadiness: Medium for intake, low for unattended ingestion
currentReality: Meta removed third-party Facebook Groups API access in 2024, including the permissions third-party tools used to fetch or publish group content. We should not build the plan around an unattended private-group feed.
whatWeCanDoNow: Build a source-capture workflow for selected posts Kenny chooses from his logged-in browser
Store internal screenshots, post links, timestamps, exact claims, author type, contradiction notes, and publish recommendations
Use the group for recurring patterns, mood, questions, and follow-up prompts, not as confirmed fact
whatNeeds: Kenny approval to store screenshots/source notes internally
A supervised capture tool or form
Optional relationship with group admins for tips/weekly prompts
privateRules: No private accusations as news without public records, named sources, or multiple credible confirmations
Default private citizens to unnamed unless the story has strong public interest
publishUse: What People Are Mad About, rumor radar, lost-and-found patterns, reader questions, source prompts, and community mood.

name: Nextdoor neighborhood signals
accessMode: Source Desk paste-and-review capture for selected notes; developer access only if approved
automationReadiness: Medium for intake, low for unattended ingestion
currentReality: Nextdoor is a core neighborhood-signal source, but its private-citizen content should enter through selected capture, not an unattended feed. Developer offerings may support approved public/local content, but access requires approval and should not be assumed to expose a private neighborhood feed.
whatWeCanDoNow: Use the Source Desk capture panel for Kenny-selected Nextdoor notes
Use it for patterns: family logistics, lost pets, service requests, safety chatter, and recurring questions
Avoid publishing private names, accusations, or screenshots from residents without independent confirmation
whatNeeds: Clarify whether Display Content access can cover East Nashville public posts/keywords
Manual capture policy if access is not approved
privateRules: No private accusations without independent confirmation
Use patterns and questions more than individual posts
firstWorkflow: Daily supervised Nextdoor pattern review through the manual capture panel
publishUse: Complaint patterns, service questions, family pain points, lost-and-found trends, and rumor prompts.

name: Reader tips and receipts
accessMode: Tips form, email, attachments, forwarded screenshots
currentReality: Tips become the safest bridge around closed platforms because readers can voluntarily send leads, screenshots, context, and corrections.
whatWeCanDoNow: Route tips into source items with attachments, risk, confidence, zone, beat, and follow-up action
Use tips to request public records checks or business/org confirmation
Ask readers to forward the weird thing instead of relying only on closed-platform access
privateRules: Anonymous tips are leads, not proof
publishUse: Scoops, questions, rumor checks, corrections, and source-backed reader prompts.
